title: Ginny and the Birth of Bonney
description: On pages 8–10, this part focuses on Unknown person #7, King Bekori, Unknown person #8, Bepo, York, Dragon as Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.
scene: Bonney discovers Kuma’s memories, revealing his Buccaneer heritage, Ginny, the Revolutionary Army, God Valley, and the painful origins behind Kuma’s sacrifice.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Then, 22 years ago, something major happened...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: The people you've kidnapped...!! Let them go!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Save us, Kumachin!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: What are you yelling about, you big oaf?! Are you trying to pick a fight with the Royal Army? That's treason, y'know!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Castle Town, the kingdom of sorbet (when Kuma was 25)

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He's apparently a pastor from some church in the Southern Province!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hey, Pastor! Didn't you hear about the new law?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We're allowed to do whatever we want to you southerners!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: You're planning on analysing them...?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: I WON'T LET THIS PASS!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: WAA AAH!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: He's a Rebel!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: My liege... this new policy...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The people might find it hard to swallow...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It is of no consequence. They'll soon grow accustomed to their new status... before long, it won't bother them. They'll all come to see the Southerners for the vermin they are.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: From this day forward, the borders of sorbet shall stop at the edge of the Northern Province!

dialogue.speaker: King Bekori
dialogue.text: Kingdom of sorbet

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The amount of celestial tribute is decided by a country's population, you see!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Kuma's church

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Castle Town

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: Old folks' village

dialogue.speaker: Bepo
dialogue.text: Lawless Land

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We'll no longer be weighed down by all the old fossils in the south!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We must cut out this cancer so the fittest of this glorious nation can thrive!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: In jail

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Tell me... what made them arrest you too?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We raised hell trying to get them to free you, kumachi...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But they gave us a thrashing in the end...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: He he he he he he he he he he he he he he he he...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Ginny... this country is on the brink of becoming a CESS- pool of bigotry and HATE...

dialogue.speaker: York
dialogue.text: King Becori is a celestial dragon puppet.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: this new policy was given to him from on high....!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: If you plan on starting a rebellion, you'll be signing up to fight the world government as well.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Looks like the man you recommended went on quite the rampage!!

dialogue.speaker: Dragon
dialogue.text: Kuma! Ginny!

dialogue.speaker: Ivankov
dialogue.text: You're going to love him, Dragon!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I SEE YOU'VE GOVE AND GOTTEN YOURSELVES CAUGHT!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Lucky for you, I VaaS IN THE NEIGH- BORHOOD!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Not way! BIG BRO?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Is that you IVA-chan?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Kuma. If you're still anything like you used to be...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...How about coming with us and changing the world?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Uwaah! Who are these guys?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: GyaH!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Your majesty, we must flee!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: As if we would let you escape! This is what you get, you Vanna-Be celestial Dragon!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The King's Been Over- Thrown! His slavery laws are no more!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The onset of the great pirate era and the tragedy of Ohara...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Thank goodness! I hated what this country was becoming!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: ...spurred these 'freedom fighters' to take up arms...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: ...against the corrupt regimes of the world,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: ...Starting numerous instructions.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: You make me sick.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And so, this mysterious band saved the kingdom of sorbet from the corruption of the world government...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It didn't take long for the two pillars of this movement to become three, as Dragon and Ivankov recruited Kuma to their cause.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: HEE- HAAW!!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: This organization would adopt a new name...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: One that echoed around the world, "The Revolutionary Army!!"

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Count me in, too!!

summary: This part advances the Kuma and Bonney’s Tragedy through 59 cleaned dialogue beats, focusing on character decisions, battlefield momentum, and the story’s larger consequences.
